string/printable.name: printable
string/printable.description: String of ASCII characters which are considered printable by Python. This is a combination of digits, ascii_letters, punctuation, and whitespace. Note By design, string.printable.isprintable() returns False. In particular, string.printable is not printable in the POSIX sense (see LC_CTYPE).

info.excerpt: String of ASCII characters which are considered printable by Python. This is a combination of digits, ascii_letters, punctuation, and whitespace. Note By design, string.printable.isprintable() returns...

string/punctuation.name: punctuation
string/punctuation.description: String of ASCII characters which are considered punctuation characters in the C locale: !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~.

info.excerpt: String of ASCII characters which are considered punctuation characters in the C locale: !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~.

string/Formatter/parse.name: parse
string/Formatter/parse.description: Loop over the format_string and return an iterable of tuples (literal_text, field_name, format_spec, conversion). This is used by vformat() to break the string into either literal text, or replacement fields. The values in the tuple conceptually represent a span of literal text followed by a single replacement field.
